questions.subject: pharmacology
questions.question: Which receptor is enhanced by benzodiazepines to produce sedative and anxiolytic effects?
questions.explanation: Benzodiazepines increase the frequency of chloride channel opening at the GABA-A receptor, enhancing inhibitory neurotransmission.

questions.subject: pharmacology
questions.question: Which antidote can reverse severe benzodiazepine-induced sedation in selected situations?
questions.explanation: Flumazenil is a benzodiazepine receptor antagonist, but it must be used carefully because it can precipitate seizures in some patients.
questions.caseScenario: A patient arrives with profound drowsiness after taking excess alprazolam.

questions.subject: pharmacology
questions.question: Barbiturates generally have a narrower therapeutic index than benzodiazepines.
questions.explanation: Barbiturates are associated with greater respiratory depression and toxicity risk, giving them a narrower therapeutic index than benzodiazepines.
